2605.27355.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
2605.27355.paper_summary: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) is the standard method to align Large Language Models (LLMs) with human preferences. In this work, we introduce alignment tampering, a potential vulnerability where the LLM undergoing alignment influences the preference dataset, causing RLHF to amplify undesired behaviors. This arises from core limitations of RLHF: (1) preference datasets are constructed from the LLM's own outputs, allowing it to influence them, and (2) pairwise comparisons only indicate which response is better, not why. These limitations can be exploited to cause alignment tampering. For example, if an LLM generates biased responses with higher quality, annotators will prefer them based on quality. However, preference labels do not distinguish quality from bias, and the reward model inherits this limitation. Optimizing such rewards through reinforcement learning or best-of-N sampling can amplify misaligned biases. Our experiments demonstrate amplification across diverse biases: from keyword bias to propaganda (e.g., sexism), brand promotion, and instrumental goal-seeking. Mitigation remains challenging, as existing techniques for robust RLHF fail to fully resolve alignment tampering without sacrificing response quality. These findings reveal structural vulnerabilities of current RLHF and emphasize the need to prevent this vulnerability. Project page: https://alignment-tampering.github.io/
2605.27355.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習來自人類反饋（RLHF）是將大型語言模型（LLMs）與人類偏好對齊的標準方法。在這項工作中，我們引入了對齊篡改，這是一種潛在的脆弱性，其中正在進行對齊的LLM影響偏好數據集，導致RLHF放大不希望的行為。這源於RLHF的核心限制：（1）偏好數據集是從LLM自身的輸出構建的，使其能夠影響這些數據集，以及（2）成對比較僅表明哪個回應更好，而不解釋原因。這些限制可以被利用來造成對齊篡改。例如，如果一個LLM生成的偏見回應質量較高，標註者將根據質量偏好它們。然而，偏好標籤並不區分質量和偏見，而獎勵模型繼承了這一限制。通過強化學習或最佳N抽樣來優化這些獎勵可能會放大不對齊的偏見。我們的實驗顯示了各種偏見的放大：從關鍵字偏見到宣傳（例如，性別歧視）、品牌推廣和工具性目標追求。減輕這一問題仍然具有挑戰性，因為現有的穩健RLHF技術未能在不犧牲回應質量的情況下完全解決對齊篡改。這些發現揭示了當前RLHF的結構性脆弱性，並強調了防止這一脆弱性的必要性。項目頁面：https://alignment-tampering.github.io/
2605.27355.author: Dongyoon Hahm et.al.
2605.27355.authors: Dongyoon Hahm, Dylan Hadfield-Menell, Kimin Lee
